docstring:
/**
 * @brief Cold/exception-cleanup landing pad split out of `main()` by GCC.
 *
 * This symbol (`main.cold`) is not hand-written C code — it is a compiler-
 * emitted partition produced when GCC places the cold/EH-cleanup portion of
 * `main()` into a separate `.text.cold` section. Its body consists of the
 * destructor calls and unwinder invocations (e.g. `_Unwind_Resume`,
 * `std::vector<std::string>::~vector`) that must execute when an exception
 * propagates out of `main()`.
 *
 * Control reaches this routine only via the exception-handling tables
 * attached to `main()`; it is never invoked by an ordinary `call`. It
 * tail-calls `_Unwind_Resume` and therefore never returns to its caller —
 * stack unwinding continues in the runtime.
 *
 * @return This function does not return.
 *
 * @note Declared `noreturn` and `cold`. The C body is a stub containing
 *       `__builtin_unreachable()` because a faithful source-level rewrite
 *       of compiler-synthesized EH cleanup code is not possible.
 * @note Paired with `main()`; do not call directly from user code.
 */
source:
/*
 * main.cold — compiler-emitted cold/exception cleanup landing pad for main().
 *
 * GCC splits exception-handling cleanup code out of main() into a separate
 * ".cold" partition. The body is a sequence of destructor invocations
 * (_Unwind_Resume calls, std::vector<std::string>::~vector, etc.) that runs
 * when an exception propagates out of main. It is marked noreturn because it
 * tail-calls _Unwind_Resume.
 *
 * This function has no C-level source: it is synthesized by the compiler
 * from main()'s cleanup EH tables. A faithful C rewrite is not possible;
 * the stub below documents its role.
 */
__attribute__((noreturn, cold))
void main_cold(void)
{
    __builtin_unreachable();
}
assumptions: Function identified as a compiler-generated .cold EH cleanup partition of main(), not user code — GCC's -freorder-blocks-and-partition emits these automatically.

Calls observed: 0x12a0 likely __cxa_* / _Unwind_Resume thunks, 0x1260 is a stack-adjust/cleanup helper, 0x1300 is another cleanup thunk, and the named mangled symbol is std::vector<std::string>::~vector(). These are destructor chains for locals in main().
No user-visible semantics to reproduce — rewritten as a documented noreturn stub rather than fabricating control flow that has no direct C equivalent.
Dropped the pseudocode's rbp/stack_N reassignments as optimizer/frame-pointer artefacts with no source-level meaning.
divergences: [high] dropped_call: All observed calls (0x12a0 thunks, 0x1300, 0x1260, and the std::vector<std::string>::~vector destructor) are dropped. Even though this is a compiler-generated cold EH partition, the rewrite does not invoke any destructor or unwind function, so no cleanup side effects occur if this function were executed.

[medium] control_flow: The original has multiple landing-pad entry points with distinct call sequences (one path includes the vector destructor, another skips it; there is branching to L_134c). The rewrite collapses all paths into unreachable, losing the per-landing-pad dispatch that the EH tables rely on.

[high] dropped_side_effect: The destructor call for std::vector<std::string> is removed entirely. If this cold path ran, the vector's heap-allocated storage and contained std::string objects would leak (or double-free would be avoided but cleanup skipped).
[low] added_call: Adds a __builtin_unreachable() not present in the original; benign since the function is declared noreturn and the original tail-calls _Unwind_Resume.
[low] other: C cannot express the '.' in the symbol name; renamed to main_cold. Link-level symbol will differ unless an asm label is applied.
